req.title: System-Admin deletes org-announcement
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to delete a org-announcement through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers a DELETE operation on /api/org-announcements by a system-admin. Five test scenarios are defined: (1) EP positive – valid admin deletes an existing announcement and gets 200/201 with entity id plus an audit record; (2) NEG – unauthenticated request receives 401; (3) DT negative – forbidden role (non-admin) receives 403; (4) BVA boundary – delete with a missing mandatory field (announcement id) receives 400; (5) STC positive – deleting an already-deleted announcement exercises the state transition from 'deleted' back to a delete attempt. The violation in test[4] was kind=negative paired with heuristic=STC, which is invalid because STC (State Transition Coverage) tests exercise system state paths and must be classified as positive (they verify correct system behaviour across states, not an error pathway). Fixing: test[4] kind changed from 'negative' to 'positive' to satisfy the rule that STC heuristic must pair with a positive kind.

tests.name: test_admin_delete_org_announcement_success
preconditions: A valid org-announcement exists and its id is retrievable via <fixture: existing_announcement_id>
actions: Send DELETE /api/org-announcements/{id} with valid admin auth token
Capture HTTP response status and body
Query audit log for the deleted announcement id via <helper_fn: get_audit_record>
expected: Response body contains the deleted announcement entity id
Audit record exists for the delete action on that entity id
trace: R-WEB-051: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-051: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_admin_delete_org_announcement_success(
    admin_auth_token,        # <fixture>
    existing_announcement_id, # <fixture>
    base_url,                # <fixture>
    get_audit_record,        # <helper_fn>
):
    url = f"{base_url}/api/org-announcements/{existing_announcement_id}"
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}"}

    resp = requests.delete(url, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert str(existing_announcement_id) in str(body), (
        "Entity id missing from response body"
    )

    audit = get_audit_record(
        entity_id=existing_announcement_id,
        action="delete",
    )
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record not written after successful delete"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_delete_org_announcement_returns_401
preconditions: A valid org-announcement id is available via <fixture: existing_announcement_id>
actions: Send DELETE /api/org-announcements/{id} with no Authorization header

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_delete_org_announcement_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but holds a non-admin role (e.g. viewer)
A valid org-announcement id is available via <fixture: existing_announcement_id>
actions: Send DELETE /api/org-announcements/{id} with a viewer-role auth token
trace: R-WEB-051: error pathway – 403 if role forbidden
R-WEB-051: actor constraint – only system-admin may delete

tests.name: test_delete_org_announcement_missing_id_returns_400
actions: Send DELETE /api/org-announcements/ with no id segment (empty/missing mandatory field)
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP status is 400 or 404 (endpoint not routed without id)
Response indicates a validation or routing error
trace: R-WEB-051: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-051: error pathway – 400 on validation error
BVA: boundary at minimum required path parameter (id absent)

tests.name: test_delete_already_deleted_org_announcement_state_transition
preconditions: An org-announcement has already been deleted; its former id is available via <fixture: deleted_announcement_id>
actions: Send DELETE /api/org-announcements/{id} for an already-deleted announcement
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP status is 404 or 400 (resource no longer exists)
Response body indicates the resource was not found or is already deleted
No duplicate audit record is written
trace: R-WEB-051: state transition – deleted -> delete attempted
STC: covers transition from terminal 'deleted' state back to a delete trigger
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_delete_already_deleted_org_announcement_state_transition(
    admin_auth_token,       # <fixture>
    deleted_announcement_id, # <fixture> – id of a pre-deleted announcement
    base_url,               # <fixture>
    get_audit_record,       # <helper_fn>
):
    url = f"{base_url}/api/org-announcements/{deleted_announcement_id}"
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}"}

    resp = requests.delete(url, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code in (400, 404), (
        f"Expected 400/404 for already-deleted id, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    # Confirm no spurious second audit record was created
    records = get_audit_record(
        entity_id=deleted_announcement_id,
        action="delete",
        return_all=True,
    )
    assert len(records) <= 1, "Duplicate audit record written for repeated delete"
